title: Add User-Defined Worksheets to IDE Editors
breadcrumbs: Add User-Defined Worksheets to IDE Editors
text: Add User-Defined Worksheets to IDE Editors To integrate custom functionality into the Uniface IDE, you can use the Worksheet Plug-In API to create forms that can be added as worksheets to IDE editors. You must then add the components to the Uniface IDE resources and configure the IDE to use them. Create a Form component that provides the functionality you want to add to an IDE editor. For more information, see Constructing Forms and Reports . Set the Form's Window Type property to contained . Implement the following mandatory operations plug-in operations: getVersion getWorksheetOptions ws_activate For more information, see Worksheet Plug-In Operations . Optionally, implement the following operations, which enable the component to respond to IDE events: ws_getsVisible ws_getsInvisible ws_objectRenamed ws_refresh For more information, see Worksheet Plug-In Operations . If the worksheet is used to modify objects, call IDE API operations to request the IDE to refresh objects and/or editors. For more information, see IDE API Operations . Optionally, package your custom components into a Uniface archive file. In the Uniface IDE assignment file, specify the location of the components in the [RESOURCES] section. You can specify a UAR, or the resources directory where the compiled components are located. For example: Explain this code [RESOURCES] ; D:/unifaceData/project/resources ; dir where IDE puts compiled components D:/uniface10/project/USERWORKSHEETS.UAR usys:ide.uar usys:ide_messages.uar Copy In the [LOGICALS] section of the Uniface IDE assignment file, define worksheet logicals to specify the editor and its custom worksheets. For example: Explain this code [LOGICALS] ;worksheet logicals IDE_DEFINE_WORKSHEETS_PRJ=MY_VIEW, MY_EXPORT IDE_DEFINE_WORKSHEETS_CPT=MY_VIEW ... Copy If you have modified a worksheet’s logical definition, the IDE detects this and restarts the editor involved. For more information, see Logicals for IDE Menu and Worksheet Plug-Ins . Note: The IDE does not detect changes made to the title and tooltip (in the getWorksheetOptions operation). If you change these, you need to restart your Editor for the changes to take effect. Related Links IDE Worksheet Plug-Ins Worksheet plug-ins are contained forms that can be added as user-defined worksheets to editors for main development objects, such as the entity editor and the component editor. It is possible to define multiple worksheets for each type of editor. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
